claim: Create specialist sub-agents with narrowly scoped responsibilities (e.g., keyword research, email marketing, retention); single-agent multitasking leads to context collapse
actionable: Design your lead agent to spin up and delegate work to specialized sub-agents rather than trying to handle all roles in one instance
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Use Opus for all agent tasks if possible—it produces more reliable outputs for multi-agent collaboration; cheaper models for single-task agents add unpredictability
actionable: Default to Opus across your agent team; pause on rate limits rather than downgrading models mid-deployment
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Never install OpenClaw on your personal computer—use a separate server, VM, or managed platform; agents can execute arbitrary commands and corrupt your system
actionable: Provision a dedicated Linux VM or cloud server for agent deployment; isolate from production and personal machines
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Create dedicated email accounts for agents; grant granular API permissions (read-only by default) to prevent unintended system changes
actionable: Set up separate Gmail/fastmail accounts for agent access; use API key controls to scope permissions (e.g., read emails, no send)
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Agents can autonomously discover and implement solutions by reading documentation, running 'doctor' checks, and self-correcting without human intervention
actionable: Give agents access to your documentation and ask them to diagnose problems; they will often fix issues without prompting for each step
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: One OpenClaw instance can manage multiple independent sub-agents via a single lead agent that routes tasks; no need for multiple hardware instances
actionable: Structure as single lead agent (Jarvis) communicating with specialized sub-agents via task assignment and shared knowledge base
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: A central dashboard (Mission Control) tracking agent conversations, tasks, and collaboration enables visibility and continuous improvement impossible with scattered Telegram chats
actionable: Build or adopt a central agent coordination dashboard where all agents log findings and sub-agents post updates for review
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Agents analyzed 100k+ emails to identify follow-up gaps and auto-drafted responses; the system quantified financial impact of missed follow-ups
actionable: Grant agent read access to email history; use it to identify business-critical oversight and quantify the cost of inaction
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Treat agents like employees: grant access incrementally (read-only first, then write to branches, finally main branch) and review outputs before trusting production changes
actionable: Implement trust-building: start with read-only access; graduate to feature branches; reserve main branch access for validated outputs
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: Agents identified root cause (poor activation, not retention) by analyzing customer behavior patterns, then auto-generated full onboarding sequences with conditional email logic
actionable: Use agents for behavioral analysis and automated workflow generation; give them access to all customer activity and historical data
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup

claim: The real bottleneck shifts from 'what should I do?' to 'which of 100 priorities should I tackle first?'—agents remove execution friction but create decision paralysis
actionable: Pair agents with clear prioritization frameworks; agents amplify your ability to execute, not your ability to decide what matters
source_title: I Built an AI Agents Army with OpenClaw to Run my $28k/mo Startup
